data.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (criterion T11 — data-migration / backfill safety overlay)

VERDICT: clean — no T11 findings. T11 is PASS / not-applicable for this plan.

affirmations:
- [T11] Correctly not-applicable per T11's own anti-FP rule ("not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory changes" / "OVERLAY — apply only when the plan changes a schema / persisted format or backfills data"). The plan introduces two NEW persisted event types (`REVIEW_RESULT` sidecars + `SIGNATURE`/attestation events) but as ADDITIVE, append-only entries to rebar's existing event log — not a modification of an existing persisted schema, not a backfill/rewrite of existing rows, and not a blocking DDL on a large table. There is no migration-EXECUTION surface (online/expand-contract, batching/throttling, resumability, dual-write window, partial-failure data-loss) for T11 to evaluate.
- [T11] The gate DEFAULTS OFF and the cert binds to a head MATERIAL-event commit; pre-existing/in-flight tickets are simply ungated rather than backfilled, so there is deliberately no data-backfill step (Success Criteria #1/#4; "rollback' is simply turning the preference off"). This is consistent with the plan's own dry-run note that rebar 'migration' is the bash->Python strangler (not a data migration) and that deterministic data-migration overlays over-fired on rebar (T11 fired 2/19, all false) — reinforcing not-applicable here.
- [T11] The genuine residual concern in this neighborhood — old+new coexistence / deploy-ordering for the new event types and mixed-version CI readers — is correctly the province of T12 (rollout/rollback/reversibility), NOT T11 (migration-execution safety); the plan routes it there. Noted only to disambiguate the two overlays; it is NOT a T11 finding.

No checklist items emitted for T11.
